commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed an Issue with placing radial decouplers in the Vehicle Editor when symmetry inheritance was enabled. It could cause a cascading failure where 1000s of decouplers would spawn causing the game to hang.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed Vehicle editor, several exceptions that could occur when placing parts with symmetry inheritance enabled.
Fixed VehicleEditor, Radial decouplers could connect to other radial decouplers.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed Launch Existing Vehicle Button was enabled when editor. Clicking it could cause multiple launch menus to open and lock up the editor.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added Rocket engine tooltip now shows required fuel type.
Changed PartTemplate.GetAllRocketTemplates() to be non-allocating.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed VehicleEditor, parts can be now flagged with muliple Diameters. Structural Part adaptors can now be filtered by either of their connector diameters.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Fixed setting of connection radially connected connector to part.
Added hotpath list of Decouplers to PartTree to be used by vehicle editor.
Added automatic vs manual stage mode in the vehicle editor (Automatic by default).
Added automatic setting of stages on parts in the vehicle editor when in automatic mode.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Fixed vulkan validation error on launch caused by the pre-pass depth validator skipping ambient occlusion and clustered lightings first frame resulting in textures not being formatted correctly initially.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Changed bool to AutomaticStageMode and reversed bool value logic.
Changed Resource Gropups UI to NOT show group info unless in manual mode.

commits.author: Morrow
lines: Reverted fix for exceptions that could occur with symmetry inheritance enabled. This is likely hiding the root cause of a problem and we'll want to find that.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Remove display of resource groups from Flight UI.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Add ability to turn visualization of fuel flow on and off per part from the part menu in the vehicle editor.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Added 'VehicleRelativeSpawn' particle updater. This is a stripped down version of VehicleAttachedMovement which runs once per particle and transforms it into the correct coordinate space. By removing the movement logic it means that the updater is not handling too many attributes, encouraging a bad workflow of creating multiple updaters for slightly different particle effects.

Reworked volumetric ice particles to now spawn on the surface of the vehicle and slowly float away from the vehicle. This is now a much more subtle effect than the original implementation. This is still a WIP particle effect and is gated behind the experimental particles toggle.
Volumetric.frag now has a hardcoded phase eccentricity, there seems to be a bug in the current implementation resulting in particles getting darker outside of a very limited range of values.
Reworked how ice particles are updated to work better with how we're using the particles.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added Engine plates now allow for symmetrical placement of engines if any symmetry option is selected. The symmetry pairs are authored in the Connector data for the Engine plates.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Added celestial min/max terrain altitude approximation. Can be used to skip work involving the terrain for vessels above this altitude, although the values are not guaranteed to be exact.
Skipped ground clutter collision data processing for vessels that are too high above the terrain.
Moved iteration over vessels for ground clutter collision data to avoid repeated work.
Improved ground clutter chunk distance checks for collision data.
Improved Mars' ground textures colouration - the diffuse textures contain a lot more contrast now.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Improved terrain material quality at kitten scales.
Reduced the frequency at which the terrain textures blend based on camera altitude.
Added the ability to teleport to anywhere on any planet to the physics debug menu. Select 'Teleport To', choose a planet and double click on the map to teleport the currently controlled vessel there.
Disabled terrain material displacement scaling up based on the current texture zoom level, since it would contribute too much to undulating vertices when the camera moved.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed VehicleEditor. Changing fuel type or clicking refill button on tank will also apply to all Symmetry partners.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed VehicleEditor. Any instance of the word sequence in the vehicle editor ui is now referred to as "Stage".
Changed Resource Groups are now behind a tab on the stage window. They are still set to automatic by default.
Changed VehicleEditor, Removed menu bar from Parts Window.
Fixed crash when editing un symmetrical part (regression form previous commit)

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed Vehicle Editor Staging Window, Hovering over parent stage elemnt now automatically highlights all items in the stage. This has replaced the Highlighted checkbox.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed copy paste error in DefaultAssets.xml. The scale icon would sometimes incorrectly appear where the angle snapping icon could be.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Adsed automatic fill logic to vehicle editor. When we attach an engine any empty tanks in the same stage will be automatically filled with that engines combustion resources.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed VehicleEditor, grabbed part now moves with the cursor when zooming or translating.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: removed Shift + e hotkey opening current craft in editor. This feature is still available through debug menu.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed Symmetries were not getting saved when saving a craft in the editor. This fix  only applies to newly created craft.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed naming of stage back to sequence, pending further design discussion.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Made the physics debug map state variables static.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Added ability to define default combustion process to a Tank template.
Added prefill logic for tanks that have a default combustion process defined when attached in the vehicle editor.
Added MMH_NTO_1.6 (RCS fuel) to the three spherical radial tanks so they are automatically prefilled with RCS fuel.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Added basic wetness value to vehicle shaders. When a vehicle falls into the ocean any parts underwater become wet. Once a vehicle is out of water it will begin to slowly dry off.
Added WIP frost effect to vehicle shaders. This is not currently visible, however the core of the effect has been implemented in MeshIndirect.frag.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Add a specular preset glint curve which is copied to all newly created vehicles. This allows newly created vehicles to have a good approximate starting curve instead of the default zero curve. The preset curve has two large peaks and produces good looking glints when tested with the current vehicles.
Save glint data on game save. This includes all glint curves for each vehicle and editor settings.

Use vehicle normals for the irregular glint curve calculation if available instead of the bounding box. Normals are currently available in the C# code for Gemini, Rocket, and newly created vehicles, but not the Kittens as the Kittens render their meshes using a different system. If normals aren't available, the bounding box calculation is used instead as a fallback. The normals allow a more accurate calculation as glints depend a lot on the variation of normals across the vehicle surface (e.g. satellites in real-life have a lot of panels with widely varying normals). The calculation is done by taking the normal variation across the x, y, and z axis of the vehicle and averaging the results into a single curve, representing the averaged axis. This final curve is able to be represented nicely as 0 - 1 values across 360 degrees, which is how the current glint curves are represented.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Fixed vulkan validation on shutdown due to frost buffer not getting disposed of properly on shutdown.
Cleaned up frost texture parameter names.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Change milky way default quality from "Medium" to "High" as there was a recent speedup of about 1.5x across all quality settings from Dan (commit hash a3438d6). There is also a significant jump in quality from Medium to High, so I felt this change would provide a lot of benefit to players.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Removed deactivate of part modules when decoupling.
Fixed loss of control inputs on split vehicles. This means engines, etc, etc will continue to burn now after a decoupling event.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Added resource groups (Stages) back into the staging UI in flight if manual resource group mode is off.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Remove old ROCKET_GLINT_ID string.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Hotfix - Use the preset specular curve instead of auto generating the irregularity curve as the auto generation is causing stutters.

commits.author: Morrow
lines: Made vehicle editor snapping align more correctly by utilizing both a forward and up vector.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Vehicle frost now supports sampling from a mask packed in the TFI_Heat textures B channel.

commits.author: Morrow
lines: Turned off face snapping when the editor is still trying to compensate for camera smoothing. This is a temporary fix for part trees rotating all over when we try to handle face snapping and compensate for camera smoothing at the same time.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: FxTemperature now goes between -1 to 1 instead of 0 to 1. Values below 0 are used to determine how frost should build up on the exterior of vehicle parts.

Added new graphics settings and category for vessel effects. This includes water, frost aswell as thin film interference and vessel ambient lighting (Which were previously part of the general graphics category). By default frost is disabled as it is still a WIP.
Temporarily removed the frost mask from MeshIndirect.frag. We currently do not have any updated textures yet with frost masks, meaning with the mask enabled no frost is visible.
Fuel Tanks are now PartModelDynamic instead of PartModels, this means that they will render with the correct temperature and frost hash defines.
Fixed heat bleeding through at lower temperature values for the vehicle frost.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Change glints to update after part tree modification so that splitting and a few other places in the code are also regenerating the glints.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Change the glint auto-irregularity curve to use the bounding box calculation.

Implement easy optimization to only compute width once in the bounding box calculation. Before applying this optimization, using a profiler revealed that we can have about 1 million parts before the bounding box calculations over all the parts add up to 0.3ms, which is where it starts becoming significant on the main thread.

commits.author: JoshRW
lines: Reword glint "Auto-generate from mesh normals" to "Auto-generate from bounding box"

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Added full disposal to RecreateResourceManagers so that they don't leak down to the GC finalizer.
Removed redundant direct call to RecreateResourceManagers in PartTree.RecomputeAllDerivedData (perf improvement).
Changed usage of memoryOwner to arrays in ResourceManagerBase - eliminates ArrayPool rent/return/clear overheads (perf improvement).
Changed temporary visited parts List to HashSet in ResourceManagerBase - Further memory usage improvement.
Changed SpanOwner to stackalloc in ResourceManager.FindReactantsNodeGroup - stack allocation so no heap hit.
Removed unnecessary UpdateAfterPartTreeModification from Vehicle class (perf improvement).

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Reworked thin film interference and frost ordering so that thin film interference is applied first and then frost acts as a layer overtop of it, instead of thin film interference applying overtop the frost.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Replaced spec constant usage with preprocessor macros in the ground clutter shaders.
Removed some logging related to ground clutter compile times.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Fixed ground clutter terrain blending not being affected by the graphics setting.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added first pass on improving connection interactions when connecting items to to fuel tanks with multiple aligned connectors. (additional data authoring is still remaining to bring most parts into this new behaviour)

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed 2m engines were not snapping to 2m internal connectors.
Added Aligned definitions for other fuel tank connectors.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Reworked the experimental volumetric particles so that they spawn based on the fuel tanks temperature. Previously the only criteria for the particles to spawn was for the fuel tank to not be empty. This also means that they are able to properly free themselves back into the emitter pool once the temperature warms back up.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed an asyymetic connection behaviour between capsules and tanks.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed Engine Plates connections not flagged as internal.
Added aligned definitions to Connectors on Engine Plates

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Reworked solid ice particles to spawn based on the fuel tanks temperature. Previously they spawned attached to the exterior of the vessel and would randomly dettach as the vessel moved, now the particles spawn already dettached as we are no longer relying on just particles for the frost effect.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Initial frost blend implementation. Instead of a linear fade in this has the frost grow across the surface of the vessel. This was implemented in still a WIP and not currently visible in game.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added Clicking deploy/retract button on a part that has symmetrical parts (i.e landing legs, solar panels) will cause all symmetrical parts to execute that action if possible.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Added basic spawn logic for water splash particles when a vehicle lands in the ocean. This is currently not tied to any particle effect and is purely just the logic to detect when to spawn particles.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Added basic water splash particle effect. This is just a placeholder particle effect that adds some basic volumetric particles on impact.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added Tooltips in Vehicle Editor for Part electrical consumption and production.
Added Force info to Tooltips in VehicleEditor for parts with a decoupler component.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Improved temperature values for fuel tanks and thrusters. Previously there was a disconnect between the thrusters and the fuel tanks temperatures. The temperatures are now alot more stable.

Added a temporary branch in MeshIndirect.frag. If a part does not have a valid Tfi_Heat texture, it defaults its frost mask to 1. Once we have proper frost masks implemented this can be removed, however for not it fixes an issue with fuel tanks not having any frost build up on them.

commits.author: GitHub
lines: Update KSA and Planet.Render.Core shader code for the current Brutal.ShaderC API. (#93)
Bump RocketWerkz.Brutal.ShaderC to 2026.7.0
Remove IsLocal conditional project references from Planet.Core and Planet.Render.Core

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed VehicleEditor, When placing tanks radially, tanks will prioritise connecting to a radial decoupler FromSurface conncector and fall back to surface snapping if a FromSurface connection can not be aquired.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Fixed Loading a game while in VehicleEditor now closes the editor.

commits.author: gravhoek-rw
lines: Fixed an error where a bogus frame conversion would cause billions of g's of gravitation when escaping from a body under full physics.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Added primitive support to MeshReference.
Added preliminary multi-mesh and multi-primitive support to ground clutter. Does not yet support multiple materials per object.
Fixed grass LOD0 sharing the same model name as a particle mesh.
Fixed ground clutter terrain blend end distance not being calculated properly.

commits.author: Linx-RW
lines: Merged primitive counts and offsets into one buffer.
Renamed 'submesh' buffers to 'primitive' to be more in line with the gltf format.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Added frost masks to CorePropulsionA and CoreFuelTankA textureAtlases.
Removed temporary shader code that was added while waiting for frost masks to be created which defaulted frost masks to always be enabled.
Minor optimization so that thin film interference only gets sampled if there is a valid tfi texture.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Cleaned up FxTemperature.cs

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added Holding middle mouse button while in editor maps mouse up/down to zoom in/out.

commits.author: Morrow
lines: Cleaned up some shared functionality in the symmetry handling in the editor.
Reverted a decoupler symmetry change to fix SymmetryLinks getting phantom entries.
Fixed an old bug related to symmetry inheritance without symmetry that caused a part to not properly get assigned a SymmetryLink in some nested cases.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Added SequenceGroups are now serialized on Part (rather than being automatically derived from Symmetry information). Any part of the same type in the same sequence can form a sequence group by dragging/dropping it onto another valid part. Parts can now also be dragged out of a sequence group.

commits.author: JPLRepoRocketWerkz
lines: Fixed issue on Linux X11 with multi-monitor layouts. The symptoms include dropdown menus and tooltips being offset when the game is being displayed on the non-main monitor. This does not affect Windows or Wayland Linux. There is a couple of side-effects of this change. Popups and tooltips can overlap the GNOME top bar or dock. Detached windows can be placed under panels.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: Ambient occlusion now uses the prepasses normal texture as an input. Previously it was recreating the normal from the depth input. This means that we now get ambient occlusion using the same normals the fragment shader uses, instead of an approximation.

commits.author: MattCaradusRW
lines: Changed Improved drag/drop interaction feel of items in sequence window in vehicle editor. The dragged part is now previewed in any potential valid sequence position.
Changed SequenceOrder (i.e. the parts index in the sequence) is now serialized  at the part level.
Chnaged Selected Diameter is retained when swapping between regions in the part window in VehicleEditor.

commits.author: Maxwell Johnson
lines: CACAO ping pong textures are now 8 bit instead of 32 bit, this saves ~0.2ms with no noticable loss in quality.

commits.author: Rocket
lines: Added new star binary, provided by community member @briarthrain. This fixes a number of problems with alignment of stars, brightness, smimmering. This is the product of intense work by them, and the community, to generate more appropriate data files for star binaries. Future update will see options for players to select more higher density ones.
